title: Initial fit does not find true minimum Combine

posts.name: Mohit Vir Srivastav
posts.text:
Dear Combine experts,
Sometimes when fitting to toys, Combine doesn’t manage to find the true minimum in the initial fit, leading to negative values for \Delta NLL. I know that when scanning one can use --pointsRandProf=N to sample N random starting points when scanning, but that does not translate to the initial fit (as I understand).
In the same spirit, is there an easy way for Combine to do the initial fit over a multidimensional space from a variety of starting points using the MultiDimFitalgorithm similar to what is described above?
Thank you,
Mohit Srivastav
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Combine experts,

Sometimes when fitting to toys, Combine doesn’t manage to find the true minimum in the initial fit, leading to negative values for \Delta NLL. I know that when scanning one can use --pointsRandProf=N to sample N random starting points when scanning, but that does not translate to the initial fit (as I understand).

In the same spirit, is there an easy way for Combine to do the initial fit over a multidimensional space from a variety of starting points using the MultiDimFitalgorithm similar to what is described above?

Thank you,
